methods.description: Process for recording phenological stages of plants according to the CTIFL scheme, including stages such as leafing-time, pistillate flower receptivity, and pollen shedding. This data is used to model plant development in relation to temperature.

steps.description: All the phenological stages were recorded as indicated in the CTIFL scheme.
steps.searchable_summary: record phenological stages according to CTIFL scheme

steps.description: The corresponding stages, when the data were collected, were the leafing-time stage, Cf; the start of pistillate flower receptivity, the Ff2 stage; the end of pistillate flower receptivity, the Ff3 stage; the start of pollen shedding, the Em stage; and the end of pollen shedding, the Hm stage.
steps.searchable_summary: record specific phenological stages: Cf, Ff2, Ff3, Em, Hm

steps.description: The data were recorded in 2-3-day intervals, usually in the morning.
steps.searchable_summary: schedule phenological data recording every 2-3 days in the morning

steps.description: The data were collected in Hungary from 2010 to 2019 and in Iran from 2006 to 2008 and from 2011 to 2019.
steps.searchable_summary: collect phenological data in Hungary and Iran over specified years

methods.description: Modeling plant development phases using Growing Degree Days (GDD) to find correlations with temperature data, aiding in the estimation of leafing-out time and other phenological phases.

steps.description: The average calendar day of the phenological data was put into the model with its related growing degree days (GDD) from 0 to 9°C at 0.1°C intervals to find the best correlation.
steps.searchable_summary: input phenological data and GDD into model to find temperature correlation

steps.description: To estimate the leafing-out time and/or blossom or other phenological phases of the grown plant species, GDD are best to use because there is a linear and direct proportional relationship between the temperature above the threshold and the rate of development of different plant organs.
steps.searchable_summary: use GDD to estimate plant phenological phases
